questions.question_text: The model shows the different layers of the Earth. Match each letter (A, B, C) to its correct layer (core, crust, mantle). [cite: 1, 5-12]
Suggest one way the model does not represent the structure of the Earth. [cite: 14]
correct_answers: There is no ocean or land visible [cite: 183]
The Earth's crust is not moving [cite: 183]

questions.question_text: Our Solar System contains planets and a star. Write down one other object found in our Solar System. [cite: 16, 17]
The Earth is one planet in our Solar System. Write down the names of two other planets in our Solar System. [cite: 19, 20]
Write down the name of the star found at the centre of our Solar System. [cite: 24]
Animals can be grouped into vertebrates and invertebrates. Write down the names of each animal in the correct group: lizard, rhino, ladybird, worm, fox, butterfly. [cite: 26-36]
Some animals have a skeleton on the outside of their body. Write down the name of this type of skeleton. [cite: 38, 39]
One function of the skeleton is to help animals move. Write down one other function of the skeleton. [cite: 41, 42]

Oliver investigates electrical conductors and insulators. Based on the results where object A (yes), B (no), C (yes), D (yes), E (no) light the lamp, write down the letters of all the objects that are electrical insulators. [cite: 48-52]
Oliver places a copper coin in the circuit. Predict what will happen to the lamp and explain your answer. [cite: 54]
correct_answers: This is because copper conducts electricity / is a good conductor. [cite: 186]

questions.question_text: Blessy adds white powder to vinegar in a beaker. Write down one way Blessy stays safe when she adds the white powder to the vinegar. [cite: 58, 63]
When the white powder reacts with the vinegar, carbon dioxide is made. Explain why this is an example of a chemical reaction. [cite: 65, 66]
correct_answers: new substance is made / gas is produced [cite: 187]

questions.question_text: Carlos draws a diagram of his science investigation with an eye, a mirror, and a light source. Draw two arrows on the diagram to show the direction light travels. [cite: 68-72]
correct_answers: one arrow from light source to mirror and one arrow from mirror to eye [cite: 188]

questions.question_text: Complete these sentences about light: Light travels in ___ lines. When light reaches the mirror the light changes direction because it is ___. [cite: 73-76]
What type of diagram has Carlos drawn? Circle the correct answer: energy diagram, light diagram, mirror diagram, ray diagram. [cite: 78-81]

A glass of water and ice cubes is left in a warm room. Complete the sentences: When ice cubes are left in a warm room the ice changes from a ___ state to a ___ state called water. This change of state is called ___. [cite: 83-88]
Describe how to change water back into ice. [cite: 90]
correct_answers: freeze the water / put it in a freezer [cite: 189]

questions.question_text: Water is a substance made from very small ___. [cite: 93]

Match the animal description to the habitat: 'Camels have large feet to stop them sinking into the sand.', 'Polar bears have thick fur to keep warm.', 'Sharks have fins to help them swim.' [cite: 99-104]
A bar chart shows the number of people infected with measles each year. How many people were infected with measles in 2016? [cite: 107-111, 120]

Vaccination is used to prevent people getting measles. Explain how the data in the bar chart supports the sentence: 'After 2016 fewer people in the country were vaccinated to prevent them getting measles.' [cite: 122-126]
correct_answers: after 2016 the number of infections increases [cite: 191]

questions.question_text: Why are safety caps important on a bottle of medicine? [cite: 130]

Objects in the home (cooker, television, lamp, food mixer) transfer electrical energy into other types of energy. Which one type of energy do all four objects transfer electrical energy into? [cite: 132-144]
Which object transfers electrical energy into movement energy? [cite: 146]
Write down the name of one object not in the pictures that transfers electrical energy to sound energy. [cite: 148]
